title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-four regulatory events across the Americas are converging on a single pressure point: stablecoin infrastructure compliance. The GENIUS Act's passage triggered parallel rulemaking at FinCEN, FDIC, and NCUA—all targeting Q3 implementation—while 16 US events include fundamental BSA/AML reform, state-level licensing frameworks, and DOJ enforcement expansion into DeFi mixers. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: Twenty-one EU member states, the UK, and six Middle Eastern jurisdictions are simultaneously moving from compliance planning to active enforcement across MiCA licensing, AML supervision, and crypto authorization frameworks. The EU's Anti-Money Laundering Authority becomes operational in Q3 with direct supervision authority over highest-risk CASPs, while France's July 1 PSAN-to-MiCA transition deadline and the UK's Q3 authorization application cutoff create urgent infrastructure buildout windows.

title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Asia-Pacific — Q2 2026
summary: Nine Asia-Pacific markets are simultaneously moving from policy consultation to active enforcement across VASP licensing, Travel Rule compliance, and institutional custody frameworks. Australia's AUSTRAC is now issuing penalties while South Korea opens traditional financial institutions to crypto services and Indonesia transfers exchange supervision to banking regulators.
